markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: The extended November 19, 2026 release date for Grand Theft Auto VI, confirmed after multiple delays from an original 2025 target and a brief May 2026 window, creates an extended timeline that shapes trader focus on high-profile events like geopolitical resolutions, major album drops, and tech milestones occurring beforehand. Rockstar Games has emphasized additional polish as the driver for the latest shift, while parent company Take-Two has repeatedly reaffirmed the date in earnings updates without further changes. This window heightens attention to marketing catalysts, including a potential second trailer rollout later in 2026, alongside broader industry patterns where lengthy development cycles for tentpole titles often coincide with shifting cultural and political landscapes that influence bettor sentiment.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Harvey Weinstein’s advanced age of 74 and deteriorating health, combined with active appeals in his California rape conviction and the unresolved New York retrial proceedings, underpin the strong trader consensus favoring no additional prison time. The 2024 overturn of his original New York conviction, followed by a hung jury in the 2025 retrial, introduced significant procedural uncertainty that continues to shape market-implied odds. Recent April 2026 developments—including the start of a third Manhattan trial on the same charges and a skeptical appellate hearing in Los Angeles—have kept momentum tilted toward potential relief or reduced effective sentencing rather than extended terms. Historical patterns in high-profile appeals and the challenges of prosecuting complex cases years later further support the current positioning around the frontrunner outcome.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Carolina Hurricanes' league-best 53-22-7 regular-season record and first-round sweep have anchored trader consensus at 55.5% implied probability, bolstered by their Eastern Conference Final lead over the Montreal Canadiens and home-ice advantage in a potential Stanley Cup Final. The Vegas Golden Knights sit at 44.1% after sweeping the Presidents' Trophy-winning Colorado Avalanche to claim the Western title, highlighting depth scoring and resilience that position them favorably against either Eastern finalist. The Canadiens trail at 1.8% as underdogs who advanced via extended series and Game 7 wins but face a series deficit and lower regular-season standing, limiting their title path in current market pricing.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping’s position as General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party and Chairman of the Central Military Commission remains firmly entrenched through 2026, supported by ongoing anti-corruption campaigns that have removed senior military figures and centralized authority without visible elite resistance or a designated successor. Recent developments, including the January 2026 investigation of top generals, February NPC expulsions, and Xi’s New Year address projecting continuity on Taiwan and regional policy, reinforce stability rather than transition pressures. High-level diplomatic engagements through mid-2026 further signal no near-term leadership shift ahead of the 2027 Party Congress. Traders assess these factors as maintaining the strong consensus against removal before the market’s December 31, 2026 resolution date.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom holds the leading position in this early-cycle market at 24.1% amid a fragmented field where no contender exceeds 25%, reflecting his established national profile as California governor, executive record, and fundraising infrastructure. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez at 10.3% draws support from progressive voters through her congressional visibility and recent indications of interest in a 2028 bid, while Kamala Harris at 8.1% benefits from name recognition tied to her post-2024 book tour and donor outreach. Recent polling averages and media assessments of the invisible primary highlight how governors and senators like Josh Shapiro and Pete Buttigieg trail due to narrower early momentum. The 2026 midterms and subsequent primary calendar could consolidate backing around frontrunners through endorsements, debate performance, and shifts in voter coalitions, though the outcome remains highly sensitive to evolving party dynamics over the next two years.
